data.description:
## Bootstrap the shared Rebar Bot signing identity

No identity ticket or SSH authorship key exists yet. Create the one the AWS + GitHub bot
environments will share.

- Generate an ed25519 keypair for the bot (private key never committed).
- Create the identity: `rebar identity create --name "Rebar Bot" --email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com --key "ssh-ed25519 …"` (the `identity` type is gate-exempt, so this bootstrap write is not blocked even under enforcement).
- Verify TOFU registration and that a `git config user.email` = `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` resolves to this identity.
- Decide/record the secure storage locations (AWS SSM `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key`; GitHub Actions secret `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY`) — provisioning happens in the per-env tasks.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Rebar Bot identity ticket exists with its ed25519 public key registered; `rebar list --type identity` shows it.
- [ ] Git-email resolution (`joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`) maps to this identity.
- [ ] Private key generated and its secure-storage destinations recorded; nothing secret committed.
data.title: Bootstrap the shared Rebar Bot signing identity (ed25519 + identity ticket)
